---
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Discussion

## Model Interpretation and Stock Status

The interpretation of the selected `r species_label` model should consider the
diagnostic evidence, data conflicts, sensitivity runs, structural uncertainty,
and implications for stock-status advice.

> **TODO:** Add the final interpretation after the diagnostic model and
> management-advice grid are accepted.

## Comparison with the Previous Assessment

The comparison with the most recent assessment should identify which changes
are caused by new data, model structure, biological assumptions, CPUE
standardisation, tagging treatment, model software, and convergence or
uncertainty treatment.

The comparison should separate changes in observed data from changes in model
assumptions. Where stock-status quantities differ materially from the previous
assessment, the report should explain whether the difference is driven mainly
by additional years of data, revised inputs, model-development decisions,
diagnostic choices, or changes in the uncertainty treatment.

> **TODO:** Compare against the previous assessment only after equivalent
> quantities are recomputed from the `r assessment_year` analysis pipeline.

## Remaining Uncertainties

Remaining uncertainties should be separated into data limitations, model
structure, biological assumptions, tagging assumptions, abundance-index
uncertainty, and computational or convergence limitations.

The uncertainty discussion should distinguish structural uncertainty,
estimation uncertainty, and optimisation uncertainty. Where models have
non-positive-definite Hessians, small negative eigenvalues, or unstable jitter
outcomes, the text should explain whether key management quantities remain
stable and whether estimation uncertainty was calculated for management advice.

> **TODO:** Replace with the final `r assessment_year` uncertainty discussion.

## Research Recommendations

> **TODO:** Update recommendations from the `r assessment_year` assessment.
> Base recommendations on the final diagnostics, data review, and uncertainty
> analysis.
